dimensionSummaries.summary: The codebase has a solid Fiori foundation — centralized CSS-variable tokens in src/index.css :root, a single StatusBadge with domain resolvers in src/lib/statusTokens.ts, ListReport/PageHeader/KpiCard primitives, and shared Modal/ConfirmDialog components with role="dialog". However adoption is inconsistent: a shared Modal exists but ZERO pages use it (17 pages hand-roll fixed-overlay modals, only 1 handles Escape, none trap focus); 226 raw bg-status-* class usages and several pages duplicate StatusBadge with hand-rolled status pill maps; ~36 hardcoded hex literals in pages (some duplicating status tokens with non-Fiori values); typography is split ~50/50 between raw Tailwind text-xs/text-sm and the 72-scale text-pm-* tokens; and shared widgets (KpiCard used in 1 page, ListReport in 2) are widely re-implemented inline. These are quality/consistency debts rather than functional breakage.

The shared design-system layer is largely solid: Button/IconButton/SortableTh/FilterBar carry focus-visible rings, index.css ships a correct global :focus-visible + high-contrast block, the Titlebar shell bar and NotificationsBell are well-labeled with Escape/outside-click handling, and StatusBadge always pairs color with a text label. The accessibility debt is concentrated in page-level ad-hoc code. The two highest-impact systemic gaps are: (1) ~235 inputs and ~76 selects have NO programmatic label association (only 5 of 129 <label> tags use htmlFor, and zero inputs/selects carry an id or aria-label), so screen readers announce no field names; and (2) ~16 page-level modals are hand-rolled fixed inset-0 divs with no role="dialog"/aria-modal/aria-labelledby, no focus trap, and backdrop close-on-click with no keyboard path, while a shared Modal component already exists. Icon-only buttons in those modals lack aria-label, and active nav items lack aria-current.

The frontend is reasonably disciplined (almost no console.log, only 7 `as any` casts, 32 `: any`), but the backend leans heavily on untyped contracts: the shared command `Handler` type is `(args: any) => any`, so all ~378 IPC handlers receive `args: any` and ~571 `: any` annotations exist across electron/server — every command's input/output is effectively unchecked. There is also genuine dead code (a `useCrudData` hook plus `useRadialZoomPan`/`useUnsavedChanges`/`memoizeAsync`/`throttle`/`deepEqual` utilities used by zero call sites) and a token-system violation where the Titlebar hardcodes `#354A5F` despite an unused `--shell-bg` token. Several pages exceed 1,000 lines with 20-39 useState calls, and PageHeader accepts title/icon/subtitle/breadcrumb props that are silently discarded while 15+ call sites still construct them.

Tooling health is fragile. Only one feature (bom-wizard) has unit tests (2 files, 65 tests passing); all 19 Zustand stores, 376 command handlers, the apiCommand API layer, lib/access page-gating, and lib/statusTokens have zero unit coverage. The app TypeScript build is currently broken: `tsc --noEmit` exits 2 on 4 TS6133 errors, which means `npm run build` (`tsc && vite build`) halts before bundling. The CI workflow is doubly broken (it runs that failing typecheck and then calls the now-deleted `build:electron` script), and it only triggers on version tags — never on PRs/pushes — so regressions land unguarded. The 5-file Playwright e2e suite still launches via `_electron`, which can no longer run because the Electron package was removed. zod is a dependency but is never imported anywhere; all 376 handlers take untyped `args: any` with no schema validation. There is no ESLint/Prettier config despite 18 `eslint-disable` comments, and dev deps are skewed (vitest 4 requires Vite ^6/7/8 but Vite 5.4.21 is installed; jsdom@29 is an unused duplicate of happy-dom).

The core "one command goes through apiCommand() against one shared registry" architecture is largely intact: 380 commands register via ipcRegister, ~256 are called through apiCommand, and only one (delete_project_piece) has a true HTTP/IPC parity gap. The Zustand stores follow a remarkably consistent fetch/mutate/optimistic pattern. The real weaknesses are: (1) a brittle dual-source-of-truth for request-body wrapping (a hardcoded client-side REQUEST_WRAPPED_COMMANDS set that must stay in lockstep with each handler's args.request reads — already drifting, with duplicates and at least one strict-wrap handler missing from the set), (2) ~9 pages reimplementing the local useState+useEffect+fetch data pattern that the unused useCrudData hook and the stores were built to eliminate, (3) inconsistent error/loading conventions (only 3 of ~13 stores expose an error field; data stores silently swallow fetch failures), and (4) stale architecture residue (Tauri/electron-updater naming and a now-dead upload-release/latest.yml feature).

findings.title: Adopt the shared Modal component — 17 pages hand-roll overlays with no Escape/focus handling
findings.evidence: grep 'fixed inset-0' → 17 page files; grep 'Escape' in pages → only ChatPage.tsx; 'Is Modal imported in pages?' → (none)
findings.issue: A correct shared Modal (src/components/Modal.tsx) exists with Escape handling and role="dialog", but it is imported by ZERO pages. Instead 17 pages (QuotationsPage, ServiceTicketsPage, ProjectsPage, ClientsPage, DeplasariPage, CalendarPage, MaintenancePage, etc.) hand-roll `<div className="fixed inset-0 bg-black/40 z-50 ...">` overlays. Only ChatPage handles the Escape key; none trap focus or set aria-modal. This is an accessibility gap (no keyboard dismiss, focus escapes to background) and a maintenance/consistency hazard.
findings.recommendation: Migrate hand-rolled overlays to the shared Modal (or a new <Dialog> wrapper). Add a focus trap + initial-focus + Escape-to-close inside Modal itself so every consumer inherits correct behavior, then replace the 17 inline `fixed inset-0` blocks. Track via grep for 'fixed inset-0 bg-black'.

findings.title: Shared Modal sets aria-modal="false" and has no focus trap
findings.evidence: aria-modal="false"  ... role="dialog" (no focus trap; comment says docks as split-view panel)
findings.issue: Even the canonical Modal declares aria-modal="false" and never moves focus into the panel or traps it. For dialogs that should capture keyboard interaction this lets focus stay on background controls and is announced incorrectly by screen readers.
findings.recommendation: Set aria-modal="true" for true modal dialogs (or expose a `modal` prop for the split-view variant), focus the first focusable element / panel on open, restore focus to the trigger on close, and trap Tab within the panel.

findings.title: Replace hand-rolled status pills with StatusBadge (FinancePage, ManagerControlPage, TabletProductionPage)
findings.evidence: FinancePage:309 `p.risk_level === 'HIGH' ? 'bg-status-red/15 text-status-red' : ...`; TabletProductionPage:19 STATUS_TONE map of bg-status-* strings; ManagerControlPage:39 critical/high/medium bg-status-* maps
findings.issue: Several pages re-implement the status-chip pattern with ad-hoc `bg-status-*/text-status-*` spans and per-file tone maps instead of <StatusBadge tone label>. TabletProductionPage uses StatusBadge 0 times despite rendering production-status pills. This diverges from the single source of truth (StatusBadge + statusTokens resolvers), risks visual drift (different padding/ring/opacity), and bypasses the genericStatus/pieceStatus resolvers.
findings.recommendation: Replace these inline pill spans/maps with <StatusBadge {...resolver(value)} /> using statusTokens resolvers (pieceStatus for production, a new riskStatus resolver for FinancePage risk_level). Add resolvers to src/lib/statusTokens.ts where missing.

findings.title: Remove hardcoded hex literals from pages — some duplicate status tokens with non-Fiori values
findings.evidence: accent: '#f59e0b' (amber), '#3b82f6' (blue), '#a855f7' (purple), '#22c55e' (green) — but --status-amber=#E9730C, --status-blue=#0A6ED1, --status-purple=#6D28D9
findings.issue: ~36 hex literals appear in src/pages. PiecesOrderingPage and PartsTree/RadialTreeNode define accent colors as raw hex that conceptually mirror status tokens but use Tailwind-default values (#3b82f6) instead of the Fiori token values (#0A6ED1), so 'blue' looks different from every StatusBadge. CLAUDE.md explicitly says never hardcode a hex in a component. (Genuinely data-driven user-picked colors in CalendarPage/SupplierCodesModal swatch lists are acceptable.)
findings.recommendation: Replace status-meaning hex with var(--status-*) (e.g. accent: 'var(--status-amber)') or read from getComputedStyle, so chart/column accents match the Belize palette. Keep only the user-pickable color palettes as literals.

findings.title: Adopt KpiCard for stat tiles — used in only 1 of ~10 pages with KPI strips
findings.evidence: InventoryPage:188 hand-rolled `<div className="... grid grid-cols-5 ..."> {metricCards.map(... text-pm-2xs uppercase ... text-base font-semibold)}`; KpiCard grep → only KanbanPage.tsx
findings.issue: src/components/ui/KpiCard.tsx (with loading skeleton, trend, hint) is reused by only one page. InventoryPage, DashboardPage, FinancePage, ManagerControlPage, PersonalTasksPage, TabletProductionPage and others re-implement KPI tiles inline with bespoke markup, producing slightly different padding/typography per page and duplicating the loading-dash logic.
findings.recommendation: Render KPI strips with <KpiCard label value loading trend hint /> instead of bespoke flex tiles. This also centralizes the loading placeholder (currently each page does its own `loading ? '—' : value`).

findings.title: Replace native window.confirm() with ConfirmDialog for Fiori consistency
findings.evidence: confirm('Sigur ștergi acest ansamblu? ...') in FisaTemplateEditor, PartsTreePage:938, SupplierCodesModal:113, CalendarPage:293, PiecesOrderingPage:179, ProjectBriefingsPage:479, FisaTemplatesPage:77, ChatPage:390
findings.issue: At least 8 destructive actions use the browser-native window.confirm()/confirm(), which renders an OS dialog that breaks the SAP Fiori look-and-feel and ignores the dark theme. The repo already has a styled ConfirmDialog component used by other pages, so the experience is inconsistent (some deletions styled, some native).
findings.recommendation: Route all destructive confirmations through the existing ConfirmDialog component (or a useConfirm hook) so they match Belize styling, theming, and Escape/focus behavior.

findings.title: Enforce the 72-typeface scale (text-pm-*) — raw Tailwind text-xs/text-sm used ~50% of the time
findings.evidence: text-xs → 343 occurrences vs text-pm-xs → 342; text-sm → 140 vs text-pm-sm → 169 across src/pages
findings.issue: Typography is split almost evenly between the raw Tailwind scale (text-xs/text-sm) and the design-token 72-scale (text-pm-xs/text-pm-sm). text-xs (12px) and text-pm-xs are close but not identical and carry different line-heights/letter-spacing, producing subtle row-height and density inconsistencies across pages that are meant to look uniform under the Fiori 72 typeface.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on the text-pm-* token scale app-wide; codemod raw text-xs→text-pm-xs / text-sm→text-pm-sm (verify each matches the intended size) and add an ESLint/grep guard to prevent new raw font-size classes in src/pages.

findings.title: Dead error state in ClientsPage — error is hardcoded to null
findings.evidence:
const error: string | null = null; ... later `if (error) { return

{error}

}`
findings.issue: ClientsPage declares `const error: string | null = null;` so the error-render branch (lines 144-150) is permanently dead — a fetch failure never surfaces an error UI on this list page; the user just sees an empty/loading state. This is a missing error-state for a primary list page.
findings.recommendation: Wire `error` to the client store's error field (the store already has loading) so a failed load renders the existing error block, and remove the hardcoded null.

findings.title: Standardize modal backdrop opacity and tokenize it
findings.evidence: grep backdrop opacity: 6× `bg-black/40`, 1× `bg-black/60`; plus SignaturePad.tsx:140 bg-black/40
findings.issue: Hand-rolled modal backdrops use inconsistent opacities (bg-black/40 vs bg-black/60) and a raw black instead of a token, so overlays look slightly different page-to-page and won't adapt to the dark theme's intended scrim.
findings.recommendation: Define a single --color-scrim / `bg-scrim` token and use it for all overlays (best done as part of the Modal migration above), removing raw bg-black/NN.

findings.title: Convert remaining list pages to ListReport where appropriate
findings.evidence: ListReport used in only InventoryPage + LibrariesPage; 18 pages still use plain <table>; QuotationsPage:157 hand-rolls loading spinner + EmptyState + sortable table
findings.issue: ListReport (PageHeader + FilterBar + sortable table + loading/empty/error states) is used by only 2 pages, while ~18 pages render plain <table> markup and re-implement their own loading spinner + empty state. Plain-list pages like QuotationsPage, DeplasariPage, DocumentsPage, WarehousePage, UserSessionsPage are good ListReport candidates and would get consistent states/sorting for free. (Dashboards, master-detail and dynamic-column pages should stay custom per CLAUDE.md.)
findings.recommendation: Migrate the genuine flat-list pages to <ListReport>; leave dashboards/master-detail/tree pages as-is. Prioritize QuotationsPage and DeplasariPage which already replicate ListReport's responsibilities by hand.

findings.title: Remove dead title/icon/subtitle props from PageHeader call sites
findings.evidence: PageHeader.tsx comment: 'the per-page TITLE ... is intentionally NOT rendered'; yet ~15 call sites pass title="..." icon={...} (ProjectsPage, InventoryPage, ClientsPage, UsersPage, AlertsPage, ServiceTicketsPage, etc.)
findings.issue: PageHeader ignores title/icon/subtitle (documented in PageHeader.tsx), but ~15 pages still pass them. The props are inert noise that misleads contributors into thinking a title renders, and several call sites (KanbanPage, WarehousePage) pass ONLY a title with no actions/tabs, meaning <PageHeader> renders nothing at all and could be removed.
findings.recommendation: Strip the dead title/icon/subtitle props from call sites; delete <PageHeader> entirely where it has no actions/tabs/back (it renders nothing). Optionally drop the props from the interface once call sites are clean.

findings.title: ExportMenu/print HTML uses hardcoded non-token colors and non-72 fonts
findings.evidence: body{font-family:system-ui,...;color:#111827} th{background:#f3f4f6} tr:nth-child(even) td{background:#fafafa}; EmailPage:298 font-family:Inter; ProcurementWorkspacePage:106 border:1px solid #ccc
findings.issue: Print/export HTML strings (ExportMenu, EmailPage, ProcurementWorkspacePage, GoodsReceiptEnhancements) hardcode hex colors and use system-ui/Inter instead of the bundled SAP 72 typeface, so printed/exported documents don't match the on-screen Belize branding.
findings.recommendation: Centralize a print stylesheet that pulls the 72 font and palette (or at least the Fiori grey #32363A / border #D9D9D9) so exports are on-brand; share one helper instead of per-file inline CSS strings.

findings.title: Associate every form input/select with a label (htmlFor/id or aria-label)
findings.evidence: <label className="block ...">Titlu *</label>  then  <input value={draft.title...} (no id / no aria-label)
findings.issue: Repo-wide, ~235 <input> and ~76 <select> elements exist but ZERO carry an id or aria-label, and only 5 of 129 <label> tags use htmlFor. Labels are merely visually adjacent, not programmatically associated. Screen readers announce 'edit text, blank' with no field name, and clicking the label text does not focus the input. This affects nearly every create/edit form in the app (tasks, deplasari, quotations, service tickets, stations, finance, kanban, parts-tree, settings).
findings.recommendation: Give each input/select an id and point its <label htmlFor> at it (or add aria-label where no visible label exists). For the common pattern, build a small <Field label> wrapper in src/components/ui that generates a useId() id and wires htmlFor automatically, then migrate forms to it to fix the whole class at once.

findings.title: Add dialog semantics and focus trapping to hand-rolled page modals
findings.evidence: <div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/60 p-4" onClick={onClose}> ... <div ... onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}>
findings.issue: About 16 page-level modals are built as raw fixed inset-0 backdrops (PersonalTasksPage x4, deplasari x3, QuotationsPage x3, ServiceTicketsPage, calendar, procurement, parts-tree SupplierCodesModal, checklist FisaTemplatePicker/Editor, ManagerControlPage). None set role="dialog", aria-modal="true", or aria-labelledby on the panel, none trap Tab focus inside the dialog, and none move focus to the dialog on open or restore it on close. Keyboard and screen-reader users can tab into the obscured page behind the modal.
findings.recommendation: Route these through a shared centered-modal primitive that sets role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId}, traps focus, focuses the first control on open, restores focus on close, and handles Escape. A Modal.tsx already exists (currently a right-docked split-view aside with aria-modal="false"); add a centered/overlay variant or a CenteredDialog sibling and migrate the ad-hoc modals to it.

findings.title: Backdrop click-to-close has no keyboard equivalent and modals miss Escape
findings.evidence: <div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-black/40 flex items-center justify-center p-4" onClick={onClose}>
findings.issue: The ad-hoc modals close only via onClick on the backdrop div (a non-interactive element with no role/tabIndex/onKeyDown) or a close button. There is no Escape handler in most of them (unlike the shared Modal.tsx and NotificationsBell which do listen for Escape). Keyboard-only users cannot dismiss via the backdrop and often cannot press Escape to close.
findings.recommendation: Centralize Escape-to-close and backdrop dismissal in the shared dialog primitive (see modal-semantics finding). Do not attach onClick to a bare div for dismissal; keep dismissal on the wrapper but always pair it with a keydown Escape listener so closing is reachable from the keyboard.

findings.title: Add aria-label to icon-only buttons in page modals and toolbars
findings.evidence: <button onClick={onClose} className="p-1 text-content-muted ..."><X className="h-4 w-4" /></button>
findings.issue: Numerous icon-only buttons render just a lucide glyph with no aria-label/title, so they announce as 'button' with no name. Confirmed cases include the modal close (X) buttons in PersonalTasksPage (lines 659, 801, 1124, 1221), QuotationsPage (458, 610, 670), ServiceTicketsPage (440), SignaturePad (144), the chat image-preview close (ChatPage 955) and new-chat/new-group close (706, 730), the ServiceTickets add-part button (350), and EmailPage trash (326). The shared IconButton component's docstring even mandates aria-label, but these hand-rolled <button>s bypass it.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-label (e.g. 'Inchide', 'Sterge', 'Adauga') to each icon-only button, or replace the hand-rolled <button><Icon/></button> with the existing <IconButton aria-label=...> primitive which already enforces focus-visible styling.

findings.title: Give CommandPalette dialog role, accessible name, and focus trap
findings.evidence: <div className="relative w-full max-w-lg mx-4 rounded-lg border border-line bg-surface-elevated ..."> (no role/aria-modal/aria-label)
findings.issue: The Ctrl+K command palette is a true centered modal portal but the dialog container has no role="dialog", aria-modal, or accessible name, and there is no focus trap (Tab can leave the palette into the page behind it). It does focus the input on open and handles Escape/arrow keys on the input, which is good, but the listbox of results is plain <button> items with no aria-activedescendant/role=option wiring to the input.
findings.recommendation: Add role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Cautare" to the dialog wrapper, trap focus within it, and optionally model the input+results as a combobox/listbox (role=combobox on input with aria-activedescendant pointing at the active option, role=listbox/option on results) so the active highlight is exposed to assistive tech.

findings.title: Label the shared FilterBar search and filter selects
findings.evidence: <input type="text" value={search} ... placeholder={searchPlaceholder} /> (no aria-label) and <select ...> with only an empty <option value="">{f.label}</option> as label
findings.issue: FilterBar is reused across many list pages (Inventory, Libraries, etc.), so its gaps multiply. The search input relies solely on a placeholder for its name (placeholders are not accessible names and disappear once typing starts), and the filter <select> has no aria-label; the empty first option acts as a visual label but a selected non-empty value leaves the control with no announced purpose.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-label={searchPlaceholder || 'Cauta'} to the search input and aria-label={f.label} to each filter <select>. Fixing it here improves every consuming list page at once.

findings.title: Mark the active workspace nav item with aria-current
findings.evidence: <span className={`absolute left-0 ... w-[3px] ... ${item.isActive ? 'bg-accent' : 'bg-transparent ...'}`} /> ; nav <button> has no aria-current
findings.issue: The currently-active page in the left navigation is conveyed only by a colored accent bar and text color (item.isActive). Screen-reader users get no indication of which nav item is current, and the distinction depends on color/contrast for low-vision users.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-current={item.isActive ? 'page' : undefined} to the nav item <button> at line 119 so assistive tech announces the active location.

findings.title: Make the account dropdown a keyboard-navigable menu
findings.evidence: <button ... aria-label="Cont" aria-expanded={menuOpen}> opens a <div> of <button>s with no role="menu"/role="menuitem", no aria-haspopup, no focus move
findings.issue: The avatar account menu sets aria-expanded but lacks aria-haspopup="menu" on the trigger and role="menu"/role="menuitem" on the popup, and does not move focus into the menu on open or support Arrow/Home/End navigation. The backdrop close-div is correctly aria-hidden, and Escape is handled, so this is a polish issue.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-haspopup="menu" to the avatar button, role="menu" to the dropdown container and role="menuitem" to each action, focus the first item on open, and support ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation. Apply the same to similar shell dropdowns for consistency.

findings.title: Distinguish unread email/message rows beyond color and weight
findings.evidence: className={`... ${!m.is_read ? 'bg-surface-secondary' : ''}`} with subject also bold-only: ${!m.is_read ? 'font-bold ...' : ...}
findings.issue: Unread state for email rows (and the chat star toggle at ChatPage/EmailPage which encodes 'starred' only via fill color) is conveyed solely through background tint and bold text. There is no text/aria affordance announcing 'unread' to a screen reader, and the bold/tint distinction can be hard to perceive for low-vision users. (NotificationsBell handles this well with an aria-label='necitit' dot, but the email/chat lists do not.)
findings.recommendation: Add an aria-label or visually-hidden text such as 'Necitit' on unread rows, and give the star toggle an aria-pressed plus aria-label='Adauga la favorite'/'Elimina din favorite' so state is exposed non-visually. Reuse the NotificationsBell unread-dot pattern.

findings.title: Add aria-label and keyboard support to the image lightbox overlays
findings.evidence: <div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-black/80 flex items-center justify-center cursor-pointer" onClick={() => setPreviewImage(null)}>  ...  <button className="absolute top-4 right-4 ..."><X className="h-6 w-6" /></button>
findings.issue: Full-screen image preview overlays (ChatPage 953, LeadDetailPage 491, MaintenancePage 306) dismiss only via backdrop onClick on a non-interactive div, with an unlabeled X close button and no Escape handler or role="dialog". Keyboard users cannot close them.
findings.recommendation: Wrap these in the shared dialog primitive (role=dialog, aria-label='Previzualizare imagine', Escape-to-close, focus trap) and add aria-label='Inchide' to the X button.

findings.title: Type the shared command Handler instead of (args: any) => any
findings.evidence: type Handler = (args: any) => any | Promise<any>;
findings.issue: The single shared command-registry Handler type is fully untyped. Because every command is registered through ipcRegister, this propagates `args: any` into all ~378 IPC handlers (grep counts 378 `args: any` in electron/ipc and 571 `: any` total in electron/server). No command input or return value is type-checked — typos in arg keys, wrong shapes, and refactors silently compile, which is the largest maintainability liability in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Make Handler generic: `type Handler<A = unknown, R = unknown> = (args: A) => R | Promise<R>;` and let ipcRegister be `ipcRegister<A,R>(name, handler: Handler<A,R>)`. Migrate handlers incrementally to declare their arg/return shapes (start with the high-traffic finance/projects/stations files). Even switching the default from `any` to `unknown` forces call sites to narrow and surfaces unchecked accesses.

findings.title: Remove dead useCrudData hook or adopt it across the 34 hand-rolled fetch pages
findings.evidence: * Generic hook for fetching a list of items from a Tauri command. * Replaces the repeated useState(data) + useState(loading) + useEffect(fetch) pattern.   ...   export function useCrudData<T>(...)
findings.issue: useCrudData exists specifically to replace the repeated `useState(data)+useState(loading)+useEffect(fetch)` pattern, yet it is imported by ZERO files while 34 pages under src/pages still roll their own loader (e.g. ProjectsPage, FinancePage, SettingsPage, StationDetailPage). It is exported from src/hooks/index.ts, making it look live. The doc comment also says 'Tauri command' — stale, this app is Electron/HTTP not Tauri.
findings.recommendation: Either delete useCrudData (and its index.ts export) as dead code, or commit to adopting it: migrate the simple list pages first to collapse the duplicated loading/error boilerplate. Fix the 'Tauri command' comment to say 'apiCommand'.

findings.title: Delete unused utility hooks and helpers (true dead code)
findings.evidence: useRadialZoomPan used in 0 files; useUnsavedChanges used in 0 files; memoizeAsync used in 0 files; throttle used in 0 files; deepEqual used in 0 files
findings.issue: Multiple exported hooks/utilities have zero usage outside their own definition: src/hooks/useRadialZoomPan.ts, src/hooks/useUnsavedChanges.ts, and exports memoizeAsync/throttle (src/lib/debounce.ts) and deepEqual/memoize (src/lib/memoization.ts, memoize used in only 1 file). With noUnusedLocals on at the module level this dead surface still ships and misleads maintainers into thinking patterns are in use.
findings.recommendation: Grep-confirm each (done) then delete the unused hooks/exports, or document why they are intentionally kept (e.g. public API). Removing them shrinks the maintenance surface and the bundle.

findings.title: Titlebar hardcodes #354A5F instead of the existing --shell-bg token
findings.evidence: className="... bg-[#354A5F] pl-2 pr-0 text-white"
findings.issue: CLAUDE.md mandates 'never hardcode a hex in a component' and src/index.css defines `--shell-bg: #354A5F` (line 96) plus a dark-theme override (line 214) precisely for this. The token is referenced by no component (`bg-shell-bg` grep = 0 hits). The Titlebar bypasses the token with `bg-[#354A5F]`, so the documented theme override for the shell bar cannot actually take effect.
findings.recommendation: Add a Tailwind color mapped to var(--shell-bg) (or use `bg-[var(--shell-bg)]`) and replace the hardcoded `bg-[#354A5F]`. This makes the dark-theme `--shell-bg` override functional and restores token-centric theming.

findings.title: PageHeader accepts title/icon/subtitle/breadcrumb props that are silently discarded
findings.evidence: interface declares title/icon/subtitle/breadcrumb but the function only destructures { onBack, children, tabs, activeTab, onTabChange }; comment: 'still accepted ... but are ignored'
findings.issue: Four props (title required, icon, subtitle, breadcrumb) are part of the public prop type but never rendered. 15+ call sites still construct them — e.g. ProjectsPage.tsx:340 `icon={<FolderKanban .../>}`, PersonalTasksPage.tsx:364 `title=... subtitle=... icon=...`. This is dead work: importing icon components, writing subtitle strings, and reading title literals that go nowhere, plus a misleading required `title` prop.
findings.recommendation: Make `title` optional (or drop title/icon/subtitle/breadcrumb from the interface entirely) and remove them from call sites in a sweep. If kept for compatibility, mark them `@deprecated` in the type so the editor flags new usages.

findings.title: Decompose 1,000+ line page and service files
findings.evidence: PartsTreePage.tsx 1355 lines / 21 useState / 8 useEffect; SettingsPage.tsx 1261 lines / 39 useState; PersonalTasksPage.tsx 1251; ProjectsPage.tsx 1163; electron/services/financeService.ts 1398 lines / 25 exports
findings.issue: Several files far exceed the 600-line guideline: 8 frontend pages >800 lines and financeService.ts (1398) / projectService.ts (1054) on the backend. SettingsPage holds 39 useState hooks in one component. These god-components/services are hard to test, review, and modify safely.
findings.recommendation: Split by concern: extract per-tab/section subcomponents and custom hooks for the largest pages (SettingsPage tabs, PartsTree canvas vs. side panels), and break financeService into invoice/payment/reporting modules. Target ~300-500 lines per unit.

findings.title: Replace `as any[]` prop type-laundering and `(n as any)` tree walks
findings.evidence: leads={leads as any[]} (QuotationsPage:222); clients={clients as any[]} (ServiceTicketsPage:183); for (const n of nodes) { ...(n as any).children... } (ProjectsPage:214)
findings.issue: A handful of frontend `as any` casts launder away real type mismatches: passing `leads as any[]`/`clients as any[]` into child components hides that the prop type and the data type disagree, and ProjectsPage walks a tree via `(n as any).children` despite a declared `{ children?: unknown[] }` type. These defeat the otherwise-clean frontend typing and will mask breakage when the underlying shapes change.
findings.recommendation: Align the child component prop types with the store/command return types so the casts can be dropped; for the tree walk, type the node as `{ children?: NodeShape[] }` and narrow with a type guard instead of `(n as any)`.

findings.title: Centralize the duplicated isAdmin role-name check
findings.evidence: const isAdmin = currentUser?.role_name === 'admin'; (also FisaTemplatesPage:46, ProjectBriefingsPage:130 & 315, SettingsPage:355/372)
findings.issue: The string literal role check `role_name === 'admin'` is repeated across at least 4 frontend files (6 occurrences), and similar literal checks recur in the backend (userService, notificationsService, escalationCron). Hardcoding the role string in many places makes a future role rename or role-model change error-prone, and it bypasses src/lib/access.ts which already centralizes page-gating.
findings.recommendation: Add a single helper (e.g. `isAdmin(user)` in src/lib/access.ts) and a backend equivalent, then replace the inline literals. Keeps the admin=1 / role_name='admin' convention in one place.

findings.title: Extract magic timing constants used in setInterval/setTimeout
findings.evidence: setInterval(fetchUnread, 60000) (App:718); setInterval(refresh, 30000) (TimeTrackerPill:43); setTimeout(()=>setStatus(null), 4000) repeated 3x in PartsTreePage (517/869/898)
findings.issue: Polling intervals and UI timeouts are scattered as bare numeric literals (60000, 30000, 4000 repeated three times in PartsTreePage, 2500, 2000, 1200, etc.). The repeated 4000ms status-clear in one file especially signals an un-extracted constant. Tuning or unifying these requires hunting through many files.
findings.recommendation: Define named constants (e.g. `UNREAD_POLL_MS`, `STATUS_CLEAR_MS`) near the relevant module or in a small timings constants file, and reuse them. At minimum collapse the three identical 4000ms timeouts in PartsTreePage into one constant/helper.

findings.title: Remove stale 'Tauri' references in comments and type docs
findings.evidence: * Standardized error response from backend (Tauri CommandError); also bom-wizard/types.ts:183/187/196/205, useCrudData.ts:19, localStorage.ts:25, commands.ts:21-23 (TauriEnvironment shim)
findings.issue: Numerous comments and a `TauriEnvironment` compatibility shim reference Tauri, but the project is an Electron-shared-backend / Express HTTP app (per CLAUDE.md, Electron itself was removed). These stale references mislead new maintainers about the actual runtime (apiCommand auto-detects Electron-IPC vs HTTP, never Tauri).
findings.recommendation: Update the comments to say 'backend command'/'apiCommand', and either remove the `TauriEnvironment` alias in src/api/commands.ts or rename it to `ElectronEnvironment` (which it already proxies). Pure documentation/clarity cleanup.

findings.title: Prefer typed catch over `catch (e: any)` in backend
findings.evidence: 17 occurrences of `catch (e: any)/catch (err: any)/catch (error: any)` across src/electron/server (e.g. serverControl.ts:146/156/169/209, server/index.ts:385/396/411/479/547)
findings.issue: CLAUDE.md work-rule #3 prefers `catch (e: unknown)` plus src/utils/errors.ts helpers, but 17 backend catch blocks still use `catch (err: any)`, accessing `.message`/`.code` without narrowing. This is inconsistent with the stated convention and the (clean) frontend error handling.
findings.recommendation: Switch these to `catch (e: unknown)` and use the existing src/utils/errors.ts helpers (or a backend equivalent) to extract message/code. Low risk, improves consistency with the documented rule.

findings.title: Wire the logger TODO or drop the placeholder
findings.evidence: // TODO: In production, send to error tracking service (Sentry, DataDog, etc.)
findings.issue: The central logger has a standing TODO to forward errors to a tracking service, with no tracking issue or owner. Left as-is it is an open-ended marker that quietly signals incomplete observability for an ERP that processes invoices/finance.
findings.recommendation: Either implement a minimal sink (or a no-op adapter with a clear extension point) or convert the TODO into a tracked issue and replace the inline comment with a reference. Removes ambiguity about whether prod error reporting exists.

findings.title: App TypeScript build is broken — 4 TS6133 errors fail `tsc` (and `npm run build`)
findings.issue: `npx tsc --noEmit` exits with code 2 because `noUnusedLocals`/`noUnusedParameters` are on and four declarations are unused: SsoButtons (src/pages/LoginEnhancements.tsx:137), ReceptionsTab (src/pages/procurement/ProcurementWorkspacePage.tsx:653), `users` (src/pages/ProjectBriefingsPage.tsx:299) and `me` (src/pages/ProjectBriefingsPage.tsx:517). The `build` script is `tsc && vite build`, so the production build never reaches the vite step. CLAUDE.md describes these as 'warnings', but they are hard errors (tsc exit 2).
findings.evidence: src/pages/ProjectBriefingsPage.tsx(299,13): error TS6133: 'users' is declared but its value is never read.  [tsc exit code 2]
findings.recommendation: Remove or actually wire up the 4 unused declarations (grep each to confirm it is truly dead before deleting, per repo rule 1). After the fix, `npx tsc --noEmit` must exit 0 so `npm run build` and CI's typecheck step pass.

findings.title: CI workflow is broken: runs failing typecheck and calls deleted `build:electron` script
findings.issue: The only workflow has three breakages after Electron removal: (1) step 'Type-check renderer' runs `npx tsc --noEmit` which currently exits 2 (see TS6133 finding); (2) step 'Build Electron app' runs `npm run build:electron`, but that script no longer exists in package.json — the step will fail immediately; (3) it also still type-checks tsconfig.electron.json and builds an electron-builder MSI/EXE for an app that no longer ships as Electron. The job can never go green.
findings.evidence: release.yml:90 `run: npm run build:electron`  — package.json has no `build:electron` script (grep returns nothing).
findings.recommendation: Rewrite the workflow for the web/server deliverable: drop the `build:electron` and electron-builder steps; replace with `npx vite build` + `tsc -p tsconfig.server.json --noEmit`. Keep `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm test` once the TS6133 errors are fixed.

findings.title: No CI gate on pull requests or pushes — only fires on version tags
findings.issue: The workflow triggers only on `push: tags: ['v*']` and manual `workflow_dispatch`. There is no `pull_request` or branch-push trigger, so typecheck/test/lint never run on day-to-day commits. Type regressions and failing tests can merge to main undetected (which is how the current broken `tsc` state persisted).
findings.evidence:
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'
  workflow_dispatch:
findings.recommendation: Add a lightweight `ci.yml` triggered on `pull_request` and `push` to main that runs (ubuntu-latest): `npm ci`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx tsc -p tsconfig.server.json --noEmit`, and `npm test -- --run`. Keep the heavyweight release build on tags only.

findings.title: Near-zero unit test coverage: stores, 376 command handlers, API layer, access/status libs all untested
findings.issue: Only 2 unit test files exist, both for src/features/bom-wizard (65 tests). Zero unit tests cover: all 19 Zustand stores in src/store/ (auth, sales, projects, materials, notifications…), the 376 commands registered via ipcRegister across 55 electron/ipc files, the apiCommand HTTP/IPC dispatch in src/api/commands.ts, the role/permission logic in src/lib/access.ts (canAccessPage/isViewerOnly/normalizeRole — security-sensitive), and the label resolvers in src/lib/statusTokens.ts. The highest-risk security logic (page gating) and the highest-churn code (command handlers) have no regression net.
findings.evidence: `find src/store src/api src/lib electron -name '*.test.ts*'` → no results; vitest run: 'Test Files 2 passed (2), Tests 65 passed (65)'.
findings.recommendation: Prioritize pure-logic tests with the highest risk-to-effort ratio: lib/access.ts (role matrix), lib/statusTokens.ts (resolver mapping), authStore + a couple of reducer-heavy stores, and the apiCommand wrap/error path. Then add handler tests for auth/permissions commands. Set a coverage floor (e.g. `vitest --coverage`) in CI to prevent backsliding.

findings.title: zod is a dependency but never used; 376 handlers take untyped `args: any` with no validation
findings.issue: zod@3.23.0 is in dependencies (and even bundled into vendor-utils in vite.config.ts) but is imported nowhere in src/, electron/, or server/ (0 matches). Meanwhile 374 handler callbacks across 54 of 55 ipc files are typed `async (args: any)` and dereference fields like `args?.username`, `args?.new_password` with no shape, type, or bounds validation. Untrusted HTTP bodies (server/commandRouter.ts) flow straight into handlers and service/SQL calls. This is both a type-safety hole and an input-validation/security gap.
findings.evidence: registry.ts: `type Handler = (args: any) => any`; auth.ts:10 `ipcRegister('login', async (args: any) => {`; grep for zod imports across src/electron/server → 0.
findings.recommendation: Either remove zod from dependencies if unused, or (preferred) introduce zod schemas at the command boundary — validate `args` in commandRouter/ipcRegister against a per-command schema and reject malformed payloads with 400 before reaching service code. Start with auth and any command that mutates roles/finance.

findings.title: Playwright e2e suite is dead — launches via `_electron` but Electron package is removed
findings.issue: All 5 e2e specs (smoke, crud, crud-crawl, deep-crawl, force-password-change; 710 lines) import from ./helpers/electronApp, which does `import { _electron as electron } from '@playwright/test'` and launches the app as an Electron binary. The electron package is no longer installed (node_modules/electron absent) and CLAUDE.md states Electron was removed. These tests cannot run, so the only end-to-end coverage is unrunnable. playwright.config.ts still defines two Electron-wiring projects.
findings.evidence: electronApp.ts:1 `import { _electron as electron, ... } from '@playwright/test'`; `ls node_modules/electron` → ELECTRON ABSENT.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite the e2e harness to drive the web app in a real browser: a Playwright `webServer` running `npm run server` on :3500 plus a chromium project navigating http://localhost:3500 (login admin/1234). Convert the electronApp helper to a page-fixture that hits the HTTP server, or delete the suite if the browser rewrite is deferred — keeping unrunnable tests is misleading.

findings.title: No ESLint/Prettier config despite 18 `eslint-disable` comments in the code
findings.issue: There is no eslint/prettier dependency, config file, or `lint`/`format` script anywhere, yet 18 source files contain `eslint-disable` comments (e.g. electron/commands/registry.ts, several services, src/App.tsx) implying a linter was once expected. Without lint, only tsc catches issues; consistency rules (no-floating-promises, no-explicit-any, import ordering) are unenforced — relevant given the pervasive `args: any` and `catch (e: any)` the repo guidelines warn against.
findings.evidence: `grep eslint|prettier package.json` → NO eslint/prettier; `grep -rl eslint-disable src electron` → 18 files.
findings.recommendation: Add ESLint (typescript-eslint, react-hooks, no-floating-promises) + Prettier with a `lint`/`format` script, and wire `npm run lint` into the proposed PR CI. This also documents/justifies the stray eslint-disable comments.

findings.title: Dev-dependency skew: vitest 4 requires Vite ^6/7/8 but Vite 5.4.21 is installed
findings.issue: package.json pins vite ^5.4.5 (installed 5.4.21) while vitest ^4.1.2 declares peerDependencies vite '^6.0.0 || ^7.0.0 || ^8.0.0'. The mismatch surfaces at test time as a stream of deprecation/conflict warnings ('CJS build of Vite's Node API is deprecated', 'esbuild option deprecated, use oxc', 'Both esbuild and oxc options were set'). The toolchain works today but is on an unsupported peer combination that can break on the next minor bump.
findings.evidence: vite installed 5.4.21; vitest peer vite '^6.0.0 || ^7.0.0 || ^8.0.0'; vitest run emits 'CJS build of Vite's Node API is deprecated' + oxc/esbuild conflict warnings.
findings.recommendation: Align the major versions: either upgrade Vite (and @vitejs/plugin-react) to v6+ to match vitest 4, or pin vitest to the 2.x/3.x line that peers with Vite 5. Re-run build + tests after alignment to clear the warnings.

findings.title: Unused duplicate test-env dependency: jsdom@29 alongside the actually-used happy-dom
findings.issue: vite.config.ts sets `test.environment: 'happy-dom'`, and happy-dom@20 is installed. jsdom@29 is also a devDependency but is referenced nowhere in config or tests (only appears in package.json and the generated licenses.json). It is dead weight (large transitive tree) and signals an abandoned/duplicated test-env choice.
findings.evidence: vite.config.ts test.environment: 'happy-dom'; grep jsdom in src/vite.config/tests → only package.json + assets/licenses.json.
findings.recommendation: Remove `jsdom` from devDependencies (or, if jsdom is the intended env, switch vite.config and drop happy-dom). Keep exactly one DOM test environment.

findings.title: apiCommand is loosely typed: free-string command name and unchecked `Promise<T>` response
findings.issue: `apiCommand<T>(command: string, requestBody?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T>` accepts any string as the command name (no union of the ~376 valid commands, so typos compile) and casts the parsed JSON response to T with no runtime validation. Combined with the server's untyped handlers, there is no end-to-end type contract: a backend response-shape change silently breaks pages at runtime instead of at compile time.
findings.evidence: src/api/commands.ts:131 `export async function apiCommand<T>(command: string, requestBody?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T>`
findings.recommendation: Generate/maintain a command-name union type and a request/response type map (ideally derived from zod schemas shared with the server), so `apiCommand` is `apiCommand<C extends CommandName>(c: C, args: Req[C]): Promise<Res[C]>`. At minimum validate critical responses (auth/session) with zod at the boundary.

findings.title: Stale tsconfig.electron.json and `Type-check electron` CI step target a removed build
findings.issue: tsconfig.electron.json still emits to dist-electron with rootDir 'electron' as if building an Electron app, and CI step 'Type-check electron' (release.yml:54) runs against it. Since Electron is gone and electron/ is now the shared backend compiled via tsconfig.server.json, this config and CI step are redundant/confusing duplication of the server typecheck.
findings.evidence: tsconfig.electron.json: `"outDir": "dist-electron", "rootDir": "electron"`; release.yml:54-55 `Type-check electron: npx tsc -p tsconfig.electron.json --noEmit`.
findings.recommendation: Drop tsconfig.electron.json (or fold electron/ entirely under tsconfig.server.json, which already includes 'electron/**/*.ts') and remove the separate CI step to avoid maintaining two overlapping backend typecheck configs.

findings.title: Register missing delete_project_piece command (HTTP/IPC parity gap)
findings.issue: pieceStore.deletePiece calls apiCommand('delete_project_piece', { id }) but no handler is registered for 'delete_project_piece' anywhere in electron/ipc or server. handleCommand() throws CommandError.notFound('No handler registered'), so deleting a piece fails at runtime. electron/ipc/pieces.ts registers create/update/bulk_import/material-requirement handlers but no piece delete.
findings.evidence: await apiCommand('delete_project_piece', { id: pieceId });
findings.recommendation: Add ipcRegister('delete_project_piece', ...) in electron/ipc/pieces.ts delegating to a PieceService.deleteProjectPiece, mirroring the other delete_* handlers (requirePositiveId(args?.id,'id') + withAuthenticatedUser). Add a quick CI check that every command string passed to apiCommand exists in the registry to catch future gaps.

findings.title: Eliminate the dual-source-of-truth for request-body wrapping
findings.issue: The client keeps a hardcoded REQUEST_WRAPPED_COMMANDS set that must stay perfectly in sync with how each server handler unwraps args (args.request vs flat args). This is two independent sources of truth for one convention and is already drifting: add_project_comment's handler reads args.request strictly with NO fallback (electron/ipc/projects.ts:91 'ProjectService.addComment(db, args.request, user)') yet 'add_project_comment' is absent from the client set — any caller wiring it through apiCommand flat would send args.request=undefined and silently break. Handlers like update_project_piece defensively use 'args?.request ?? args' to paper over the drift, proving the convention is unreliable.
findings.evidence: const REQUEST_WRAPPED_COMMANDS = new Set([ ... ]); function wrapIfNeeded(command, body) { if (!REQUEST_WRAPPED_COMMANDS.has(command)) return body ?? {}; ... return { request: body }; }
findings.recommendation: Pick ONE convention. Simplest: always wrap as { request: body } client-side and have every handler read 'args.request ?? args', or never wrap and migrate handlers to read flat args. Then delete REQUEST_WRAPPED_COMMANDS entirely. Until then, audit every handler that reads args.request without a fallback against the client set.

findings.title: Remove duplicate entries in REQUEST_WRAPPED_COMMANDS
findings.issue: create_finance_invoice, update_invoice_status, and record_invoice_payment are each listed twice (lines 83 and 102) in the REQUEST_WRAPPED_COMMANDS Set. Harmless at runtime (Set dedupes) but is dead duplication and a tell-tale sign the list is maintained by hand without dedup checks — exactly the brittleness called out above.
findings.evidence: line 83 'create_finance_invoice','update_invoice_status','record_invoice_payment'  ... line 102 'create_finance_invoice','update_invoice_status','record_invoice_payment'
findings.recommendation: Delete the duplicate trio on line 102. If keeping the set, add an ESLint no-dupe or a build-time assertion on the source array length vs Set size.

findings.title: Adopt useCrudData / stores in pages that hand-roll the fetch pattern
findings.issue: Nine pages (DeplasariPage, ProjectsPage, PersonalTasksPage, SalesHubPage, LeadDetailPage, LibrariesPage, MaintenancePage, ServiceTicketsPage, WarehousePage) reimplement the exact useState(data)+useState(loading)+useCallback(fetch)+useEffect pattern that the useCrudData hook (src/hooks/useCrudData.ts) was explicitly written to replace. useCrudData has ZERO usages across the repo, so the abstraction exists but is dead. This duplicates loading/error handling logic 9+ times and diverges from the Zustand-store approach used by the other ~26 pages.
findings.evidence: const fetch = useCallback(() => { setLoading(true); apiCommand<Deplasare[]>('get_deplasari').then(...).catch(()=>setDeplasari([])).finally(()=>setLoading(false)); }, []);
findings.recommendation: Either route these list pages through useCrudData (or a dedicated store, matching domain pages like clientStore/salesStore), or delete useCrudData if the store pattern is the chosen standard. Standardize on ONE data-fetching primitive for list pages.

findings.title: Standardize fetch-error/loading conventions across stores
findings.issue: Data stores (project, client, sales, piece, handoff, material, station, dashboard) silently swallow fetch failures — they console.error and return cached/empty data with no error state, so the UI cannot distinguish 'no data' from 'fetch failed'. Only authStore, alertStore, and toastStore expose an 'error' field. Mutations rethrow while fetchers swallow, an inconsistent contract. clientStore is also internally inconsistent: updateClient does an optimistic set() then refetch, deleteClient does optimistic set() with no refetch, createClient does neither (just refetch).
findings.evidence: catch (err) { console.error('[clientStore] fetchClients failed:', err); set({ loading: false }); return get().clients; }
findings.recommendation: Add an optional error: string|null field to the data stores' state (set on catch, cleared on success) so pages can surface a retry/error banner, mirroring authStore/alertStore. Pick one mutation strategy (optimistic+refetch) and apply it uniformly within each store.

findings.title: Retire the dead electron-updater upload-release feature
findings.issue: CLAUDE.md states Electron and HTTP auto-update were removed ('HTTP auto-update — moot now Electron is gone'), yet server/releaseUpload.ts still exposes POST /api/admin/upload-release that regenerates latest.yml specifically for electron-updater, and AboutPanel.tsx's ReleasePublisher widget uploads installers promising 'clients download automatically at the next 30-min check'. With no Electron client, no consumer reads latest.yml — this is an orphaned feature and a raw non-apiCommand HTTP surface (one of the few legitimate-looking-but-actually-dead endpoints).
findings.evidence: server/releaseUpload.ts:4 'a regenerated latest.yml ... (electron-updater' ; AboutPanel.tsx:113 'POSTs a .exe / .msi to /api/admin/upload-release'
findings.recommendation: Confirm with stakeholders, then remove server/releaseUpload.ts route + the ReleasePublisher widget in AboutPanel.tsx (and PROMIX_UPDATES_DIR plumbing) if web-only is permanent. If desktop packaging may return, gate the feature behind a flag and clearly mark it dormant.

findings.title: Purge stale Tauri / desktop-only naming and dead BOM-wizard commands
findings.issue: Multiple active files still describe the API in terms of 'Tauri command' (the long-abandoned original stack), creating misleading mental models for a wouter/Express codebase. useCrudData's doc says 'fetching a list of items from a Tauri command'; commands.ts keeps a TauriEnvironment alias; src/features/bom-wizard/types.ts references read_excel_file / import_bom / get_proiecte_lista 'Tauri command' responses — but import_bom and get_proiecte_lista are not registered anywhere (only read_excel_file exists), so those types describe non-existent commands.
findings.evidence: useCrudData.ts:19 'Generic hook for fetching a list of items from a Tauri command.' ; types.ts:205 'Project list item from get_proiecte_lista Tauri command'
findings.recommendation: Replace 'Tauri command' wording with 'command' / 'apiCommand', drop the TauriEnvironment alias if unused, and delete or update the bom-wizard type comments that reference unregistered import_bom/get_proiecte_lista commands.

findings.title: Mirror server-derived status logic in client risks drift
findings.issue: deriveStatusFromStageId() is an explicit hand-maintained copy of the server's deriveStatusFromStageId in projectService.ts (the comment says so), hardcoding stage_id->status mappings (1='ofertă', 8='livrare', 9='finalizat', etc.) for optimistic UI. Business logic duplicated across the client/server boundary will silently drift the next time production stages change, showing wrong optimistic statuses until the refetch corrects them.
findings.evidence: // Map stage_id → status (mirror of deriveStatusFromStageId in projectService.ts). ... case 8: return 'livrare'; case 9: return 'finalizat';
findings.recommendation: Either have the server return the resolved status in the production_board/projects payload so the client never recomputes it, or expose the stage->status mapping from a single shared command (e.g. get_project_stages already carries order/ids) and derive from that data rather than a hardcoded switch.

findings.title: Adopt a consistent command-naming convention (mixed RO/EN)
findings.issue: Registered command names mix English and Romanian domain terms: create_aviz, create_bon_consum, list_avize_by_project, list_bon_consums_by_project, get_deplasari sit alongside fully-English siblings (create_invoice, get_projects, create_material_consumption). The mixed/pluralization-inconsistent naming (deplasari vs avize vs bon_consums) makes the ~380-command surface harder to discover and grep, and there is no documented convention.
findings.evidence: registered: create_aviz, create_bon_consum, list_avize_by_project, list_bon_consums_by_project, get_deplasari (vs get_projects, create_invoice)
findings.recommendation: Document a naming convention (verb_noun, English or Romanian — pick one for new commands) in CLAUDE.md. Existing names are baked into the client API so leave them; just stop the bleed by enforcing the convention on new ipcRegister calls in review.
